steps.title: Keep so linked to that
steps.content: The sentence uses a coordinated adjective series: "so bright, so localized, and so dependable that it has become known as Maracaibo's Lighthouse." In a so-that construction, the adjective after "so" should stay directly connected to the result clause beginning with "that."

steps.title: Remove separating marks
steps.content: Choice A inserts a colon, Choice B inserts a semicolon, and Choice D inserts a comma. Each mark incorrectly separates "dependable" from "that it has become known." The sentence needs no punctuation after the adjective. The answer is C.
